[[ZH:志磨神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17212913}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shima Shrine |Image = [[File:Shima-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}677 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|14|24.79|N|135|10|51.18|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shima Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = Kisansho Shrine |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Shima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} [[File:Shima-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shima Shrine'' (Shima Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City), {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q28693031|WD=Q28693031|1=Shizuhi Shrine}} (Wada, Wakayama City, {{jal ink|Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743}}Setssha) is called ``Kinosanshoshya''{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined.The notation and pronunciation of the enshrined deities are based on the shrine history book and the explanation board within the precincts. . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto'' (Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto) - Another name is {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto). ; Deity enshrined :* Ikudama no Kami - The history of his enshrinement is unknown {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. As a different theory, ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}'' states that ``Kisansho Shrine'' enshrines the three deities of Idakeso (Itake no Mikoto, Oyatsuhime no Mikoto, and Tomatsuhime no Mikoto), and assumes that the deity enshrined at Shima Shrine is Oyatsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. However, since Shima Shrine is located on Kawanakajima in {{ill|lt=Kinokawa|Q1042624|WD=Q1042624|1=Kinokawa River}}, Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto is appropriate from the location point of view {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. Also, in "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}," Funatama Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} Setsusha is said to be the deity of the Ki clan and the headquarters of ``Shima-kami, Shizuka-kami, and Date-kami'' {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === Our company was designated and renamed Shima Shrine "Shima Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} years ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}) {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. Currently, as the place name ``{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}'' indicates, it is located on an island in the {{ill|lt=Kinokawa|Q1042624|WD=Q1042624|1=Kinokawa River}} delta, but the company name ``Shima'' is said to refer to Kawanakajima.{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. Before Hiden, our company was called ``Kuzu Myojin (Kunizu Myojin),'' and at the time of the Genna era, there were six small shrines on the island, including our company, but Kuzu Myojin was chosen because it had the best location.{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}} === Outline === In ancient literature, in the ``{{ill|lt=Shinsho Kokufusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'' {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), {{ill|lt=Kobe (Minbe)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe|1=Kanbe (social class)}} was the name of the island god. There is a description of ``Seven Houses'' {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The existence of Kobe can also be seen in the mention of "Island Kobe" in Nagusa District in "{{ill|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}" {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}Among the Kisansho shrines, Shimagami is the only one where Kobe is mentioned. {{Harv|Shima Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. . In national history, it is known as ``Shima-kami'' along with Date-kami and Shizuka-kami. (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} In 11 years ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}), he was given the lower rank of Seigoi, and in {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}}3 years ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}) he was given the rank of junior fourth rank and lower. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), he was promoted to Seishiinoge, Jogan 17th year ({{jalink|875|Q {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}} which contains an article about the promotion of Junior Third Rank (Junior Third Rank|Q11591025}}).{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} "Shima Shrine" In addition to being listed as ``Meishin University'' and listed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, it is also listed as ``Shoichii Shima Okami'' in ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}''{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}.Also, Shima Shrine is {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City), {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q28693031|WD=Q28693031|1=Shizuhi Shrine}} (Wada, Wakayama City), and is said to have been called "Kinosanshoshya" {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The description of "Kisanshosha" is found in the storage rice book of {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549|1=Eishō (Heian period)}}3 ({{ill|lt=1048|Q19359|WD=Q19359|1=1048}}), as well as in "{{ill|lt=middle right record|Q11363231|WD=Q11363231|1=Chūyūki}}" ink|Tennin|Q1193599}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1109|Q19515|WD=Q19515|1=1109}}), "{{ill|lt=Ryojin Hisho|Q3010584|WD=Q3010584|1=Ryōjin Hishō}}" appears to be a four-phrase divine poem {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. In "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}", about the origin of Kisansho Shrine, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} {{jalink|Sankan Conquest} It is said that {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}} was enshrined in {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} for the three ships used in the raid|Q11249435}}.The location of the ``Shima Shrine'' mentioned in the above documents was later unknown, but it was identified in the Genwa period ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}) to our company, which was then called ``Kuzu (Kunizu) Myojin'' {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. According to "{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}," our company was established for {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} years ({{ill|lt=1596|Q6785|WD=Q6785|1=1596}}-{{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}) It used to have five fields of rice fields as its shrine territory, but it was confiscated during land survey, and then it was rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Kishu Tokugawa family|Q11605046|WD=Q11605046|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}} {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}17 ({{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625|1=1942}}) In {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Shikiuchi shrine)|1987}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} November 3, 11 years ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}), from the lower junior fifth rank to the lower upper fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Zoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'') - The notation is ``Shima God''. ** October 21, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} ({{ill|lt=850 years|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}), Junior Fourth Rank Lower (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Shima God''. ** {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} January 27, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from the lower rank of the fourth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - The notation is ``Shima God''. ** October 17, 17th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), from the upper fourth rank to the third rank of junior rank (``Nihon Sandai Jirroku'') - Written as ``Shima no Kami.'' * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}'') - Written as ``Shima Okami''. === Precincts === A stone lantern with the inscription of {{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}}6 ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}) has been erected within the shrine grounds.{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Festival descriptions are from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1032 Shima Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). . * Star Festival Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Summer Festival (Yoimiya July 14th, Hongu Shrine July 15th) *Children's mikoshi togyo (2nd Saturday of July) * Annual festival (October 15th) * Niwakuri ritual (night of December 15th) === Cultural assets === === Cultural property designated by Wakayama Prefecture === *Tangible cultural property ** Shima Shrine Main Hall (with 26 ridge tags, 1 pair of lion and guardian dog) (building) - Designated on March 18, 2022 {{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/010100/kenpou/d00209691_d/fil/r0403180295.pdf From Wakayama Prefectural Newsletter, March 18, 2020] Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 1 (link to Wakayama Prefecture homepage). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}677 '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West Japan) {{ill|lt=Kise Main Line|Q582803|WD=Q582803|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Kiwa Station|Q3527469|WD=Q3527469|1=Kiwa Station (Wakayama)}} (about 5 minutes walk) * Bus: {{ill|lt=Wakayama Bus|Q11417290|WD=Q11417290|1=Wakayama Bus}} “Shima Jinja-mae” (The bus stop name is “Ma” instead of “Ma”) '''Surroundings''' * Jugosha Shrine (Matsushima, Wakayama City) - According to the legend of Jugosha Shrine, Shima Shrine was originally enshrined in this area. . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book, precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Chihiro Shima|chapter=Shima Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakukan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Shima Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese Historical Place Name System|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 31 Wakayama Prefecture place name|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}edited|author=Kentori Maruyama|year=1986|chapter=Shima Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 6 Ise/Shima/Iga/Kii|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=456002216X|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Shima-jinja}} * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1032 Shima Shrine] - [[ZH:志磨神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17212913}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shima Shrine |Image = [[File:Shima-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}677 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|14|24.79|N|135|10|51.18|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shima Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = Kisansho Shrine |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Shima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} [[File:Shima-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shima Shrine'' (Shima Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City), {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q28693031|WD=Q28693031|1=Shizuhi Shrine}} (Wada, Wakayama City, {{jal ink|Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743}}Setssha) is called ``Kinosanshoshya''{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined.The notation and pronunciation of the enshrined deities are based on the shrine history book and the explanation board within the precincts. . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto'' (Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto) - Another name is {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto). ; Deity enshrined :* Ikudama no Kami - The history of his enshrinement is unknown {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. As a different theory, ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}'' states that ``Kisansho Shrine'' enshrines the three deities of Idakeso (Itake no Mikoto, Oyatsuhime no Mikoto, and Tomatsuhime no Mikoto), and assumes that the deity enshrined at Shima Shrine is Oyatsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. However, since Shima Shrine is located on Kawanakajima in {{ill|lt=Kinokawa|Q1042624|WD=Q1042624|1=Kinokawa River}}, Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto is appropriate from the location point of view {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. Also, in "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}," Funatama Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} Setsusha is said to be the deity of the Ki clan and the headquarters of ``Shima-kami, Shizuka-kami, and Date-kami'' {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === Our company was designated and renamed Shima Shrine "Shima Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} years ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}) {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. Currently, as the place name ``{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}'' indicates, it is located on an island in the {{ill|lt=Kinokawa|Q1042624|WD=Q1042624|1=Kinokawa River}} delta, but the company name ``Shima'' is said to refer to Kawanakajima.{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. Before Hiden, our company was called ``Kuzu Myojin (Kunizu Myojin),'' and at the time of the Genna era, there were six small shrines on the island, including our company, but Kuzu Myojin was chosen because it had the best location.{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}} === Outline === In ancient literature, in the ``{{ill|lt=Shinsho Kokufusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'' {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), {{ill|lt=Kobe (Minbe)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe|1=Kanbe (social class)}} was the name of the island god. There is a description of ``Seven Houses'' {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The existence of Kobe can also be seen in the mention of "Island Kobe" in Nagusa District in "{{ill|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}" {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}Among the Kisansho shrines, Shimagami is the only one where Kobe is mentioned. {{Harv|Shima Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. . In national history, it is known as ``Shima-kami'' along with Date-kami and Shizuka-kami. (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} In 11 years ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}), he was given the lower rank of Seigoi, and in {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}}3 years ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}) he was given the rank of junior fourth rank and lower. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), he was promoted to Seishiinoge, Jogan 17th year ({{jalink|875|Q {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}} which contains an article about the promotion of Junior Third Rank (Junior Third Rank|Q11591025}}).{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} "Shima Shrine" In addition to being listed as ``Meishin University'' and listed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, it is also listed as ``Shoichii Shima Okami'' in ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}''{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}.Also, Shima Shrine is {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City), {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q28693031|WD=Q28693031|1=Shizuhi Shrine}} (Wada, Wakayama City), and is said to have been called "Kinosanshoshya" {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The description of "Kisanshosha" is found in the storage rice book of {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549|1=Eishō (Heian period)}}3 ({{ill|lt=1048|Q19359|WD=Q19359|1=1048}}), as well as in "{{ill|lt=middle right record|Q11363231|WD=Q11363231|1=Chūyūki}}" ink|Tennin|Q1193599}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1109|Q19515|WD=Q19515|1=1109}}), "{{ill|lt=Ryojin Hisho|Q3010584|WD=Q3010584|1=Ryōjin Hishō}}" appears to be a four-phrase divine poem {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. In "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}", about the origin of Kisansho Shrine, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} {{jalink|Sankan Conquest} It is said that {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}} was enshrined in {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} for the three ships used in the raid|Q11249435}}.The location of the ``Shima Shrine'' mentioned in the above documents was later unknown, but it was identified in the Genwa period ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}) to our company, which was then called ``Kuzu (Kunizu) Myojin'' {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. According to "{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}," our company was established for {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} years ({{ill|lt=1596|Q6785|WD=Q6785|1=1596}}-{{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}) It used to have five fields of rice fields as its shrine territory, but it was confiscated during land survey, and then it was rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Kishu Tokugawa family|Q11605046|WD=Q11605046|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}} {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}17 ({{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625|1=1942}}) In {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Shikiuchi shrine)|1987}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} November 3, 11 years ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}), from the lower junior fifth rank to the lower upper fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Zoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'') - The notation is ``Shima God''. ** October 21, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} ({{ill|lt=850 years|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}), Junior Fourth Rank Lower (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Shima God''. ** {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} January 27, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from the lower rank of the fourth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - The notation is ``Shima God''. ** October 17, 17th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), from the upper fourth rank to the third rank of junior rank (``Nihon Sandai Jirroku'') - Written as ``Shima no Kami.'' * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}'') - Written as ``Shima Okami''. === Precincts === A stone lantern with the inscription of {{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}}6 ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}) has been erected within the shrine grounds.{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. File:Shima-jinja honden.JPG|Main shrine (Wakayama prefecture designated cultural property) covered roof File:Shima-jinja shinmon.JPG|Shimamon === Setsusha === * Amaterasu Shrine, Benzaiten Shrine, Ebisu Shrine * Kushima Shrine, Usa Hachiman Shrine, Yasaka Shrine * Kunizu Shrine, Tenman Shrine, Kotohira Shrine * Inari Shrine, Tsukuyomi Shrine, Iso Shrine * Yakihi Shrine, Sarutahiko Shrine File:Shima-jinja keidaisha-1.JPG|Shima-jinja keidaisha-1.JPG|Shima-jinja keidaisha-1.JPG| File:Shima-jinja keidaisha-2.JPG|Shima-jinja keidaisha-2.JPG|Shima-jinja keidaisha-2.JPG| === Festival === The main festivals are as follows. Festival descriptions are from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1032 Shima Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). . * Star Festival Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Summer Festival (Yoimiya July 14th, Hongu Shrine July 15th) *Children's mikoshi togyo (2nd Saturday of July) * Annual festival (October 15th) * Niwakuri ritual (night of December 15th) === Cultural assets === === Cultural property designated by Wakayama Prefecture === *Tangible cultural property ** Shima Shrine Main Hall (with 26 ridge tags, 1 pair of lion and guardian dog) (building) - Designated on March 18, 2022 {{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/010100/kenpou/d00209691_d/fil/r0403180295.pdf From Wakayama Prefectural Newsletter, March 18, 2020] Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 1 (link to Wakayama Prefecture homepage). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}677 '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West Japan) {{ill|lt=Kise Main Line|Q582803|WD=Q582803|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Kiwa Station|Q3527469|WD=Q3527469|1=Kiwa Station (Wakayama)}} (about 5 minutes walk) * Bus: {{ill|lt=Wakayama Bus|Q11417290|WD=Q11417290|1=Wakayama Bus}} “Shima Jinja-mae” (The bus stop name is “Ma” instead of “Ma”) '''Surroundings''' * Jugosha Shrine (Matsushima, Wakayama City) - According to the legend of Jugosha Shrine, Shima Shrine was originally enshrined in this area. . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book, precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Chihiro Shima|chapter=Shima Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakukan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Shima Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese Historical Place Name System|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 31 Wakayama Prefecture place name|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}edited|author=Kentori Maruyama|year=1986|chapter=Shima Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 6 Ise/Shima/Iga/Kii|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=456002216X|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Shima-jinja}} * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1032 Shima Shrine] - Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Office * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520101.html Shima Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Collection” {{Shinto horizontal}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shima Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shima Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Wakayama]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名しましんしや]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines of Wakayama]]

It is located in [[Kii Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo [[ZH:志磨神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17212913}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Shima Shrine |Image = [[File:Shima-jinja haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}677 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|14|24.79|N|135|10|51.18|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shima Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = Kisansho Shrine |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919|1=October 15}} |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Shima Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} [[File:Shima-jinja torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Shima Shrine'' (Shima Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City), {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q28693031|WD=Q28693031|1=Shizuhi Shrine}} (Wada, Wakayama City, {{jal ink|Kamayama Shrine|Q11597743}}Setssha) is called ``Kinosanshoshya''{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. === Enshrined God === The following two deities are enshrined.The notation and pronunciation of the enshrined deities are based on the shrine history book and the explanation board within the precincts. . ; Main enshrined deity :* '''Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto'' (Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto) - Another name is {{ill|lt=Ichikishima Hime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima Hime no Mikoto). ; Deity enshrined :* Ikudama no Kami - The history of his enshrinement is unknown {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}. As a different theory, ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}'' states that ``Kisansho Shrine'' enshrines the three deities of Idakeso (Itake no Mikoto, Oyatsuhime no Mikoto, and Tomatsuhime no Mikoto), and assumes that the deity enshrined at Shima Shrine is Oyatsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. However, since Shima Shrine is located on Kawanakajima in {{ill|lt=Kinokawa|Q1042624|WD=Q1042624|1=Kinokawa River}}, Nakatsushima Hime no Mikoto is appropriate from the location point of view {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. Also, in "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}," Funatama Shrine in the {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine|Q705949|WD=Q705949|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} Setsusha is said to be the deity of the Ki clan and the headquarters of ``Shima-kami, Shizuka-kami, and Date-kami'' {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. === History === Our company was designated and renamed Shima Shrine "Shima Shrine" in the {{ill|lt=Genna (Japan)|Q635245|WD=Q635245|lt=Genna|1=Genna}} years ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}) {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. Currently, as the place name ``{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}'' indicates, it is located on an island in the {{ill|lt=Kinokawa|Q1042624|WD=Q1042624|1=Kinokawa River}} delta, but the company name ``Shima'' is said to refer to Kawanakajima.{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. Before Hiden, our company was called ``Kuzu Myojin (Kunizu Myojin),'' and at the time of the Genna era, there were six small shrines on the island, including our company, but Kuzu Myojin was chosen because it had the best location.{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}} === Outline === In ancient literature, in the ``{{ill|lt=Shinsho Kokufusho|Q11502312|WD=Q11502312|1=Shinshō Kyakuchokufushō}}'' {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido|1=Daidō}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021|1=806}}), {{ill|lt=Kobe (Minbe)|Q11589711|WD=Q11589711|lt=Kobe|1=Kanbe (social class)}} was the name of the island god. There is a description of ``Seven Houses'' {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The existence of Kobe can also be seen in the mention of "Island Kobe" in Nagusa District in "{{ill|lt=Wamyo Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}" {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}Among the Kisansho shrines, Shimagami is the only one where Kobe is mentioned. {{Harv|Shima Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}. . In national history, it is known as ``Shima-kami'' along with Date-kami and Shizuka-kami. (Japan)|Q1075605|Jōwa}} In 11 years ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}), he was given the lower rank of Seigoi, and in {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}}3 years ({{ill|lt=850|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}) he was given the rank of junior fourth rank and lower. (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), he was promoted to Seishiinoge, Jogan 17th year ({{jalink|875|Q {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}} which contains an article about the promotion of Junior Third Rank (Junior Third Rank|Q11591025}}).{{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|lt=extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927 years|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}}) {{jali nk|Engishiki Shrine Name Book|Q11064932|Shin Name Book}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053|1=Kii Province}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547|1=Nagusa district}} "Shima Shrine" In addition to being listed as ``Meishin University'' and listed in {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}, it is also listed as ``Shoichii Shima Okami'' in ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}''{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}.Also, Shima Shrine is {{ill|lt=Date Shrine (Wakayama City)|Q11380847|WD=Q11380847|lt=Date Shrine|1=Itate Shrine}} (Sonobe, Wakayama City), {{ill|lt=Shizuka Shrine|Q28693031|WD=Q28693031|1=Shizuhi Shrine}} (Wada, Wakayama City), and is said to have been called "Kinosanshoshya" {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The description of "Kisanshosha" is found in the storage rice book of {{ill|lt=Eisho|Q1193549|WD=Q1193549|1=Eishō (Heian period)}}3 ({{ill|lt=1048|Q19359|WD=Q19359|1=1048}}), as well as in "{{ill|lt=middle right record|Q11363231|WD=Q11363231|1=Chūyūki}}" ink|Tennin|Q1193599}} 2nd year ({{ill|lt=1109|Q19515|WD=Q19515|1=1109}}), "{{ill|lt=Ryojin Hisho|Q3010584|WD=Q3010584|1=Ryōjin Hishō}}" appears to be a four-phrase divine poem {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. In "{{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha Jindaiki|Q11381815|WD=Q11381815|1=Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine Chronicles}}", about the origin of Kisansho Shrine, {{ill|lt=Empress Jingu|Q232803|WD=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} {{jalink|Sankan Conquest} It is said that {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}} was enshrined in {{ill|lt=Takeuchi Sukune|Q1073668|WD=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}} for the three ships used in the raid|Q11249435}}.The location of the ``Shima Shrine'' mentioned in the above documents was later unknown, but it was identified in the Genwa period ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}-{{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}) to our company, which was then called ``Kuzu (Kunizu) Myojin'' {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}. According to "{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964|1=Kii Zoku Fudoki}}," our company was established for {{ill|lt=Keicho|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} years ({{ill|lt=1596|Q6785|WD=Q6785|1=1596}}-{{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}) It used to have five fields of rice fields as its shrine territory, but it was confiscated during land survey, and then it was rebuilt by the {{ill|lt=Kishu Tokugawa family|Q11605046|WD=Q11605046|1=Kishū Tokugawa family}} {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|WD=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}17 ({{ill|lt=1942|Q18625|WD=Q18625|1=1942}}) In {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, it was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Shikiuchi shrine)|1987}}. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jōwa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa (Heian period)}} November 3, 11 years ({{ill|lt=844|Q23835|WD=Q23835|1=844}}), from the lower junior fifth rank to the lower upper fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Zoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}'') - The notation is ``Shima God''. ** October 21, 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|WD=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} ({{ill|lt=850 years|Q30364|WD=Q30364|1=850}}), Junior Fourth Rank Lower (``{{ill|lt=Nihon Montoku Emperor Annals|Q1052905|WD=Q1052905|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}'') - Written as ``Shima God''. ** {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} January 27, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897|1=859}}), from the lower rank of the fourth rank to the lower rank of the fourth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}'') - The notation is ``Shima God''. ** October 17, 17th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=875|Q23816|WD=Q23816|1=875}}), from the upper fourth rank to the third rank of junior rank (``Nihon Sandai Jirroku'') - Written as ``Shima no Kami.'' * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008|1=Kii no Kuni Jinmyō-chō}}'') - Written as ``Shima Okami''. === Precincts === A stone lantern with the inscription of {{ill|lt=Oei|Q1193078|WD=Q1193078|1=Ōei}}6 ({{ill|lt=1399|Q6422|WD=Q6422|1=1399}}) has been erected within the shrine grounds.{{Sfn|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}. Festival descriptions are from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1032 Shima Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). . * Star Festival Setsubun Festival (February 3rd) * Summer Festival (Yoimiya July 14th, Hongu Shrine July 15th) *Children's mikoshi togyo (2nd Saturday of July) * Annual festival (October 15th) * Niwakuri ritual (night of December 15th) === Cultural assets === === Cultural property designated by Wakayama Prefecture === *Tangible cultural property ** Shima Shrine Main Hall (with 26 ridge tags, 1 pair of lion and guardian dog) (building) - Designated on March 18, 2022 {{PDFlink|[https://www.pref.wakayama.lg.jp/prefg/010100/kenpou/d00209691_d/fil/r0403180295.pdf From Wakayama Prefectural Newsletter, March 18, 2020] Wakayama Prefectural Board of Education Notice No. 1 (link to Wakayama Prefecture homepage). . === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747|1=Wakayama (city)}}{{ill|lt=Nakanoshima (Wakayama City)|Q17226981|WD=Q17226981|lt=Nakanoshima|1=Nakanoshima}}677 '''Access''' * Railway: {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} (JR West Japan) {{ill|lt=Kise Main Line|Q582803|WD=Q582803|1=Kisei Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Kiwa Station|Q3527469|WD=Q3527469|1=Kiwa Station (Wakayama)}} (about 5 minutes walk) * Bus: {{ill|lt=Wakayama Bus|Q11417290|WD=Q11417290|1=Wakayama Bus}} “Shima Jinja-mae” (The bus stop name is “Ma” instead of “Ma”) '''Surroundings''' * Jugosha Shrine (Matsushima, Wakayama City) - According to the legend of Jugosha Shrine, Shima Shrine was originally enshrined in this area. . === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Notes''' {{reflist|group="Note"}} '''Source''' {{reflist}} === References === * Shrine history book, precinct information board * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikiuchisha Research Group|author=Chihiro Shima|chapter=Shima Shrine|year=1987|title=Shikiuchisha Investigation Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakukan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Shima Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Japanese Historical Place Name System|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 31 Wakayama Prefecture place name|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{ill|lt=Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596|WD=Q2392596|1=Ken'ichi Tanigawa}}edited|author=Kentori Maruyama|year=1986|chapter=Shima Shrine|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 6 Ise/Shima/Iga/Kii|publisher={{ill|lt=Hakusuisha|Q4346470|WD=Q4346470|1=Hakusuisha Publishing}}|isbn=456002216X|ref={{Harvid|Shima Shrine (Gods)|1986}}}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Shima-jinja}} * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1032 Shima Shrine] -
